summary:
The week of February 23 to March 1, 2026, saw escalating Middle East tensions culminate in dramatic developments, overshadowing other global conflicts. On March 1, Israeli airstrikes killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, aged 86. Iran retaliated with missile and drone attacks on Israel and four Gulf Arab countries. Tehran accused the US and Israel of a school strike near a military site that killed 108 people. US Congress members demanded a war powers vote to restrain President Trump from attacking Iran. These events built on US-Iran indirect talks held on February 26 in Geneva amid warship deployments, marking the third round.

Sudan's crisis persisted with a UN mission on February 24 and 26 concluding the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) siege of El Fasher bore hallmarks of genocide. Drones intensified attacks on Kordofan gold and oil regions on February 25. In eastern Congo, Rwanda-backed rebels overran troops and entered Goma safe-haven city on February 24.

Ukraine faced setbacks: Hungary vetoed an EU 90 billion euro loan and new Russia sanctions on February 23 (faded quickly). Russia launched major strikes ahead of US-Ukraine talks in Geneva on February 27. EU Chief Ursula von der Leyen visited Kyiv on February 24 for the fourth anniversary of Russia's invasion.

NATO mounted its first coordinated response to suspected Baltic Sea cable sabotage on February 23, developing into action after the cable was severed on February 25. Pakistan's Defense Minister declared open war on Afghanistan on February 27 following cross-border strikes.

US forces captured a third Venezuelan oil tanker on February 25, with Trump claiming receipt of 80 million barrels. SpaceX launched four astronauts on February 26 to retrieve a NASA crew stuck nine months on the ISS. China's Vanke reported huge losses on February 26, signaling property crisis spread.

A 6.8-magnitude earthquake struck off Sabah, Malaysia, on February 24, the strongest in 11 years. Stories fading quickly included Mexico's raid killing cartel leader El Mencho (62 dead, February 23), UK arrest of ex-US Ambassador Peter Mandelson over Epstein ties, and Panama occupying two Hong Kong-run Canal ports. Anthropic's CEO rejected Pentagon demands to remove AI safeguards on February 27 amid contracts dispute. No major resolutions occurred; escalations dominated. (378 words)
